title: Malty Molten German Semolina Flour Gratin
description: A creamy, cheesy German-inspired dish featuring semolina flour, perfect for a cold evening. The malty flavor of the semolina pairs beautifully with the rich flavors of the cheese and spices.
instructions: In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the semolina flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, until lightly toasted.
Gradually add the heavy cream, whisking constantly to avoid lumps. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it thickens.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the cheddar cheese, mozzarella cheese, Dijon mustard, bhuna curry powder, salt, and black pepper. Let the mixture cool slightly.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and top with grated Parmesan cheese.
Place the baking dish under the raclette grill and cook for 15-20 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the gratin is set.
Remove the gratin from the grill and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Slice the gratin into portions and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve immediately, while the cheese is still molten and bubbly.
